intro.title: Start With A Short Tour
intro.intro: This Bilibili video walks through sayori.org: the blog, whiteboard, guestbook, public services, project entries, and why they live on the same desk.

profile.name: Amiya_desi
profile.intro: Amiya_desi is the public identity I keep for long-running personal work: Godot games, personal websites, homelab notes, Cloudflare workflows, Obsidian writing systems, AI-assisted development notes, tools, posts, and retrospectives. This entry keeps the stable public pieces together: blog posts, GameJam projects, GitHub repositories, GeoScore, a small whiteboard, the public services guide, a guestbook, and public contact links. I use AI for debugging, retrieval, refactoring, docs, and retrospectives, but treat it as an amplifier rather than a substitute for the work itself.

columns.title: Public Directions
items: Game development: Godot 4.x, GDScript, and C# for 2D prototypes, state machines, event buses, object pools, resources/save systems, and editor tooling.
Gameplay expression: platformer movement, time rewind, input buffering/coyote time, level pacing, feel tuning, and meta-leaning narrative puzzle prototypes.
Publishing surface: personal site, blog, Bilibili, GitHub, GameJam projects, and public digital services, slowly turning the portfolio into a growing doorway.
Web / homelab: static sites, blog content pipelines, Linux, Docker, Cloudflare Pages / Tunnel, and ops scripts.

items.text: WakeUpAtTheBorder: Godot 2D narrative platformer about a border-crossing premise, 2nd place in GodotHub 3rd Dev Contest.
TimeRuner: C# action platformer built around 5-second rewind and compact level pacing, 034GameJam best time-themed game.
EnhanceSaveSystem: Godot save plugin focused on maintainable save engineering: AES-GCM, gzip, atomic writes, and migrations.
Mio's Windowanderer / Masquerade of Girls: multi-window puzzle and text-puzzle GameJam work.

learning: Boundaries and retrospectives for AI-assisted development

items.question: What is sayori.org?
items.answer: It is Amiya_desi's personal public entry point for the blog, Godot game projects, public tools, homelab and Cloudflare notes, Obsidian workflows, GitHub, Bilibili, and contact links.

items.question: What does the blog cover?
items.answer: blog.sayori.org covers tutorials, homelab notes, Godot development, AI-assisted development retrospectives, resource indexes, project logs, and some personal daily echoes.

items.question: Is this a commercial service or local business site?
items.answer: No. This is a personal public identity and work entry. It does not publish fake addresses, phone numbers, price lists, local service pages, or commercial delivery promises.

items.question: How can I contact or learn more?
items.answer: Use me@sayori.org, or continue through the public links on this page: GitHub, Blog, Bilibili, Linux.do, GeoScore, the whiteboard, the public services guide, and the guestbook.

services.title: Public services
services.intro: The home panel keeps only the common entries. The full guide explains current status, operating boundaries, and small invitation-only plans.

items.title: Full services guide

items.title: GeoScore
items.desc: A public SEO / GEO audit tool with factual checks, an Evidence Map, and verifiable repair tasks.

items.title: Amiya Whiteboard
items.desc: A lightweight browser whiteboard for quick sketches, rough diagrams, and explaining ideas without an account.

items.title: Public writing
items.desc: Tutorials, site notes, Godot development, project retrospectives, and an RSS feed.

items.title: Search Gateway
items.desc: An open-source unified search and evidence snapshot project. The hosted instance is private and has no public API quota.

items.title: Guestbook & feedback
items.desc: Leave a note, report a service issue, or share what worked for you.

services.footnote: Paused experiments and private infrastructure are not listed as public entries.
